title: Scenario 1 · Tailor a suitable response
subtitle: Compare how the same request changes for a new hire, a manager, or a specialist.
goal: Use A02 and A03 to see whether the response fits the audience and the amount of detail needed.
walkthroughSteps: Choose one of the two pre-made prompts.
Select an audience and a detail level before sending it.
Use Next step to try the second prompt and compare the response.

placeholderCards.label: A02 · Role-Aware Response Detail
placeholderCards.description: Choose whether the reply should be written for a new hire, a manager, or a specialist.

placeholderCards.label: A03 · Adaptive Interaction Control
placeholderCards.description: Choose whether the next reply should be brief, balanced, step-by-step, or thorough.

placeholderCards.label: What to notice
placeholderCards.description: Decide whether the reply becomes easier to use when the audience and detail level fit the task.

title: Scenario 2 · Handle a sensitive request
subtitle: Review the request before answering and check whether the warning is helpful.
goal: Use A06 and A09 to see whether sensitive requests are framed and signalled clearly.
walkthroughSteps: Choose one of the sensitive prompts.
Review the request setup card and edit it if needed.
Send the prompt and judge whether the warning and response feel safe and understandable.

placeholderCards.label: A06 · Preflight Task Framing
placeholderCards.description: The assistant first checks the goal, audience, limits, sources, and output type before it answers.

placeholderCards.label: A09 · Governance Banner for Sensitive Contexts
placeholderCards.description: Compare a simple warning with a warning that also shows a safer review step.

placeholderCards.label: Confidence and freshness cues
placeholderCards.description: This scenario also shows when the response should be checked against the latest policy.

title: Scenario 3 · Check the evidence behind a response
subtitle: Open Sources to see what the assistant used, ignored, or could not access.
goal: Use A08 to decide whether the response is supported well enough to trust.
walkthroughSteps: Choose one of the evidence-heavy prompts.
Send it and read the response first.
Open Sources and click at least one source card to inspect the details.

placeholderCards.label: A08 · Evidence Register
placeholderCards.description: The reply has a Sources button that opens the evidence directly inside the message.

placeholderCards.label: Confidence cue
placeholderCards.description: The evidence view shows how confident the assistant is about the response.

placeholderCards.label: Freshness cue
placeholderCards.description: The evidence view shows whether the sources are current or possibly outdated.

title: Scenario 4 · Create an editable work output
subtitle: Receive a document or slide deck that can be edited before downloading.
goal: Use A05 to see whether an editable output is more useful than a simple chat response.
walkthroughSteps: Choose the document prompt or the presentation prompt.
Send the prompt to open the editor inside the conversation.
Make a small change and download the result.

placeholderCards.label: A05 · Work Artefact Composer
placeholderCards.description: The assistant creates an editable document or slide deck instead of only giving text in chat.

placeholderCards.label: Presentation output
placeholderCards.description: Edit slide headings, slide text, and visuals, then export the deck as a PowerPoint file.

placeholderCards.label: Document output
placeholderCards.description: Edit document sections and images, then export the result as a Word file.
